stories.title: Initialize Repository Structure
stories.description: As a content creator I want to have a complete folder structure with all required directories and placeholder files so that Claude Code can immediately navigate and operate within the system.
acceptance_criteria: BRAND/ directory exists with voice.md, pillars.md, style.md, do-dont.md, audience.md
BRAND/visuals/ directory exists with palette.md, typography.md
BRAND/visuals/components/ directory exists
BRAND/examples/ directory exists
BACKLOG/ directory exists with ideas.yaml, series.yaml, parking-lot.md
PROJECTS/ directory exists
PROMPTS/ directory exists with all prompt files
PROMPTS/skills/ directory exists
scripts/ directory exists
scripts/fixtures/ directory exists
Makefile exists
README.md exists
.github/workflows/ directory exists
stories.test_command: test -d BRAND && test -f BRAND/voice.md && test -f BRAND/pillars.md && test -d PROMPTS/skills && test -d scripts/fixtures && test -f Makefile && test -f README.md && echo 'PASS'

stories.title: Initialize Astro Site
stories.description: As a content creator I want to have a working Astro site in SITE/ so that content can be built and previewed locally.
acceptance_criteria: SITE/package.json exists and contains astro in dependencies
SITE/package.json contains tailwindcss in dependencies
SITE/tailwind.config exists
npm install completes with exit code 0
npm run build completes with exit code 0
SITE/dist/ directory is created after build
stories.test_command: cd SITE && grep -q '"astro"' package.json && npm install && npm run build && test -d dist && echo 'PASS'

stories.title: Migrate Existing Content Collections
stories.description: As a content creator I want to have my existing talks, portfolio, and publications migrated to Astro content collections so that my existing content is preserved and accessible.
acceptance_criteria: SITE/src/content/talks/ directory exists with .md files
SITE/src/content/portfolio/ directory exists with .md files
SITE/src/content/publications/ directory exists with .md files
SITE/src/content/config.ts defines schemas for talks, portfolio, publications, blog
SITE/public/artifacts/legacy/ directory exists
Site builds without errors
stories.test_command: cd SITE && test -d src/content/talks && test -d src/content/portfolio && test -f src/content/config.ts && test -d public/artifacts/legacy && npm run build && echo 'PASS'

stories.title: Configure GitHub Actions Deployment
stories.description: As a content creator I want to have automated deployment to macegrim.github.io so that publishing is a single command.
acceptance_criteria: .github/workflows/deploy.yml exists
Workflow triggers on push to main branch
Workflow uses DEPLOY_TOKEN secret (not hardcoded)
README.md documents DEPLOY_TOKEN setup
stories.test_command: test -f .github/workflows/deploy.yml && grep -q 'DEPLOY_TOKEN' .github/workflows/deploy.yml && grep -q 'DEPLOY_TOKEN' README.md && echo 'PASS'

stories.title: Create Voice Guidelines
stories.description: As Claude Code I want to read comprehensive voice guidelines from BRAND/voice.md so that all generated content matches Mason's distinctive voice.
acceptance_criteria: voice.md contains Tone section with slider settings
voice.md contains all four signature moves with examples
voice.md contains Taboos section listing AI hype, savior framing, performative futurism
voice.md contains Nuance section explaining when taboo topics ARE okay
voice.md contains at least 3 example sentences demonstrating voice
stories.test_command: grep -q 'Tone:' BRAND/voice.md && grep -q 'Systems thinking' BRAND/voice.md && grep -q 'Taboos:' BRAND/voice.md && grep -q 'Nuance:' BRAND/voice.md && echo 'PASS'

stories.title: Create Content Pillars Document
stories.description: As Claude Code I want to read content pillars from BRAND/pillars.md so that generated ideas align with strategic positioning.
acceptance_criteria: pillars.md contains Enterprise AI lane with Audience field
pillars.md contains Tribal AI lane with Audience field
pillars.md contains Deep Dives lane with Audience field
pillars.md contains Experiments lane with Audience field
pillars.md contains section on how content supports speaking positioning
stories.test_command: grep -q 'Enterprise AI' BRAND/pillars.md && grep -q 'Tribal AI' BRAND/pillars.md && grep -q 'speaking' BRAND/pillars.md && echo 'PASS'

stories.title: Create Audience Profiles
stories.description: As Claude Code I want to read audience profiles from BRAND/audience.md so that content targets the right readers.
acceptance_criteria: audience.md defines primary audience with Goals, Pain points, Looking for sections
audience.md mentions CTO, VP Engineering, or AI leads
audience.md defines secondary audience (practitioners)
audience.md defines tertiary audience (tribal communities)
stories.test_command: grep -q 'Goals:' BRAND/audience.md && grep -qE '(CTO|VP|decision-maker)' BRAND/audience.md && grep -q 'tribal' BRAND/audience.md && echo 'PASS'

stories.title: Create Visual Style Guide
stories.description: As Claude Code I want to read visual guidelines from BRAND/visuals/ so that generated artifacts match Mason's aesthetic.
acceptance_criteria: palette.md defines at least 3 colors with hex codes
typography.md specifies heading and body font families
Three visual modes defined: playful, technical, minimal
stories.test_command: test $(grep -cE '#[0-9A-Fa-f]{6}' BRAND/visuals/palette.md) -ge 3 && grep -q 'heading' BRAND/visuals/typography.md && echo 'PASS'

stories.title: Create Ideation Prompt
stories.description: As Claude Code I want to have clear instructions in PROMPTS/10-ideate.md so that I can generate on-brand ideas.
acceptance_criteria: 10-ideate.md begins with instruction to read BRAND files first
10-ideate.md explains web search can be used for zeitgeist topics
10-ideate.md defines scoring criteria: brand_fit, novelty, artifact_potential, effort
10-ideate.md shows exact YAML format for ideas.yaml entries
stories.test_command: grep -q 'Read BRAND' PROMPTS/10-ideate.md && grep -q 'web search' PROMPTS/10-ideate.md && grep -q 'ideas.yaml' PROMPTS/10-ideate.md && echo 'PASS'

stories.title: Seed Initial Ideas Backlog
stories.description: As a content creator I want to have 5-10 starter ideas in ideas.yaml so that I can immediately select one to work on.
acceptance_criteria: ideas.yaml is valid YAML
ideas.yaml contains at least 5 idea entries
Each entry has required fields: id, title, lane, effort, hook, audience, artifacts, status, created
Ideas span at least 2 different content lanes
At least 1 idea each of small, medium, and large effort
stories.test_command: python3 -c "import yaml; data=yaml.safe_load(open('BACKLOG/ideas.yaml')); assert len(data)>=5; print('PASS')"

stories.title: Create Studio-Ideate Skill
stories.description: As a content creator I want to run /studio-ideate to generate new ideas so that my backlog stays fresh.
acceptance_criteria: Skill definition exists in PROMPTS/skills/studio-ideate.md
Setup script scripts/setup-skills.sh exists
Skill content references reading BRAND files first
Skill content references updating ideas.yaml
stories.test_command: test -f PROMPTS/skills/studio-ideate.md && test -f scripts/setup-skills.sh && grep -q 'BRAND' PROMPTS/skills/studio-ideate.md && echo 'PASS'

stories.title: Create Drafting Prompt
stories.description: As Claude Code I want to have clear instructions in PROMPTS/30-draft.md so that I can scaffold complete project folders.
acceptance_criteria: 30-draft.md explains project folder structure: brief.md, outline.md, draft.md, artifacts/, review/
30-draft.md defines curiosity mode: shorter, single artifact
30-draft.md defines deep mode: longer, multiple artifacts
30-draft.md references BRAND/voice.md for tone guidance
stories.test_command: grep -q 'brief.md' PROMPTS/30-draft.md && grep -q 'curiosity' PROMPTS/30-draft.md && grep -q 'deep' PROMPTS/30-draft.md && echo 'PASS'

stories.title: Create Studio-Draft Skill
stories.description: As a content creator I want to run /studio-draft <idea-id> to scaffold a project so that I can start writing immediately.
acceptance_criteria: Skill definition exists in PROMPTS/skills/studio-draft.md
Skill references creating PROJECTS/<slug>/ directory
Skill references creating brief.md, outline.md, draft.md
Skill references updating idea status in ideas.yaml
stories.test_command: test -f PROMPTS/skills/studio-draft.md && grep -q 'PROJECTS' PROMPTS/skills/studio-draft.md && grep -q 'brief.md' PROMPTS/skills/studio-draft.md && echo 'PASS'

stories.title: Create Artifact Prompt
stories.description: As Claude Code I want to have clear instructions in PROMPTS/40-artifacts.md so that I can generate working visualizations.
acceptance_criteria: 40-artifacts.md explains three options: p5.js, D3.js, Canvas
40-artifacts.md references BRAND/visuals/ for style templates
40-artifacts.md states requirement: artifact must render without console errors
40-artifacts.md defines graceful degradation: include static fallback image
stories.test_command: grep -q 'p5.js' PROMPTS/40-artifacts.md && grep -q 'D3' PROMPTS/40-artifacts.md && grep -q 'fallback' PROMPTS/40-artifacts.md && echo 'PASS'

stories.title: Create Artifact Testing Script
stories.description: As Claude Code I want to run scripts/test-artifact.sh <path> so that I can verify visualizations work before proposing them.
acceptance_criteria: scripts/test-artifact.sh exists and is executable
Script uses Playwright to load HTML file
Script returns exit code 0 if no console errors
Script returns exit code 1 if console errors found
Test fixtures exist: scripts/fixtures/good-artifact.html, scripts/fixtures/bad-artifact.html
stories.test_command: test -x scripts/test-artifact.sh && test -f scripts/fixtures/good-artifact.html && test -f scripts/fixtures/bad-artifact.html && scripts/test-artifact.sh scripts/fixtures/good-artifact.html && ! scripts/test-artifact.sh scripts/fixtures/bad-artifact.html && echo 'PASS'

stories.title: Create Reusable Visualization Components
stories.description: As Claude Code I want to have template components in BRAND/visuals/components/ so that I can scaffold visualizations quickly.
acceptance_criteria: BRAND/visuals/components/p5-template.html exists with working p5.js setup
BRAND/visuals/components/d3-template.html exists with working D3.js setup
Each template includes usage instructions as HTML comments
Templates pass artifact testing
stories.test_command: test -f BRAND/visuals/components/p5-template.html && test -f BRAND/visuals/components/d3-template.html && scripts/test-artifact.sh BRAND/visuals/components/p5-template.html && echo 'PASS'

stories.title: Create Review Prompt
stories.description: As Claude Code I want to have clear instructions in PROMPTS/50-review.md so that I can run quality checks.
acceptance_criteria: 50-review.md defines checklist items: voice_consistency, technical_accuracy, artifacts_work, has_hook, brand_pillars
50-review.md explains how to write review/checklist.md
50-review.md explains how to write review/edits.md
50-review.md states: never modify draft.md directly
stories.test_command: grep -q 'voice_consistency' PROMPTS/50-review.md && grep -q 'checklist.md' PROMPTS/50-review.md && grep -q 'never modify draft' PROMPTS/50-review.md && echo 'PASS'

stories.title: Create Studio-Review Skill
stories.description: As a content creator I want to run /studio-review <slug> to check a draft so that I catch issues before publishing.
acceptance_criteria: Skill definition exists in PROMPTS/skills/studio-review.md
Skill references running artifact tests
Skill references creating review/checklist.md
Skill explicitly states: do NOT modify draft.md
stories.test_command: test -f PROMPTS/skills/studio-review.md && grep -q 'checklist.md' PROMPTS/skills/studio-review.md && grep -qE '(NOT|never|do not).*(modify|edit|change).*draft' PROMPTS/skills/studio-review.md && echo 'PASS'

stories.title: Create Repurpose Prompt
stories.description: As Claude Code I want to have clear instructions in PROMPTS/60-repurpose.md so that I can generate platform variants.
acceptance_criteria: 60-repurpose.md explains LinkedIn format: professional tone, 1300 character limit
60-repurpose.md shows structure: hook, key insight, call to action
60-repurpose.md specifies output location: platforms/linkedin.md
stories.test_command: grep -q 'LinkedIn' PROMPTS/60-repurpose.md && grep -q '1300' PROMPTS/60-repurpose.md && grep -q 'platforms/' PROMPTS/60-repurpose.md && echo 'PASS'

stories.title: Create Publish Prompt
stories.description: As Claude Code I want to have clear instructions in PROMPTS/70-publish.md so that I can move content to the site.
acceptance_criteria: 70-publish.md explains pre-check: verify review/checklist.md has all items passing
70-publish.md explains file move: draft.md to SITE/src/content/blog/<slug>.mdx
70-publish.md defines required MDX frontmatter: title, description, pubDate, tags, lane
70-publish.md explains error handling if build fails
stories.test_command: grep -q 'checklist.md' PROMPTS/70-publish.md && grep -q 'SITE/src/content/blog' PROMPTS/70-publish.md && grep -q 'frontmatter' PROMPTS/70-publish.md && echo 'PASS'

stories.title: Create Studio-Publish Skill
stories.description: As a content creator I want to run /studio-publish <slug> to publish a post so that content goes live with minimal friction.
acceptance_criteria: Skill definition exists in PROMPTS/skills/studio-publish.md
Skill references verifying review checklist passed
Skill references moving files to SITE/
Skill references updating idea status to published
stories.test_command: test -f PROMPTS/skills/studio-publish.md && grep -q 'checklist' PROMPTS/skills/studio-publish.md && grep -q 'SITE' PROMPTS/skills/studio-publish.md && grep -q 'published' PROMPTS/skills/studio-publish.md && echo 'PASS'

stories.title: Create System Prompt
stories.description: As Claude Code I want to read core operating rules from PROMPTS/00-system.md so that I follow the right patterns in every session.
acceptance_criteria: 00-system.md states rule: Read BRAND folder before any generation
00-system.md states rule: Never overwrite Mason's edits
00-system.md states rule: Prefer presenting options over asking open-ended questions
00-system.md states rule: Test artifacts before proposing
stories.test_command: grep -q 'Read BRAND' PROMPTS/00-system.md && grep -q 'Never overwrite' PROMPTS/00-system.md && grep -q 'options' PROMPTS/00-system.md && echo 'PASS'

stories.title: Create Makefile with Common Commands
stories.description: As a content creator I want to run common operations via Make so that commands are discoverable and consistent.
acceptance_criteria: make help target exists
make setup target exists
make preview target exists
make build target exists
make deploy target exists
make test target exists
make validate target exists
stories.test_command: grep -q 'help:' Makefile && grep -q 'setup:' Makefile && grep -q 'preview:' Makefile && grep -q 'deploy:' Makefile && grep -q 'test:' Makefile && echo 'PASS'

stories.title: End-to-End Test with Example Post
stories.description: As a content creator I want to have one complete example post so that I can verify the entire workflow works.
acceptance_criteria: PROJECTS/example-post/ directory exists
PROJECTS/example-post/brief.md exists with Scope, Audience, Key Points sections
PROJECTS/example-post/draft.md exists with complete post content
PROJECTS/example-post/artifacts/ contains at least one working visualization
PROJECTS/example-post/review/checklist.md exists with all items marked [x]
SITE/src/content/blog/example-post.mdx exists with valid frontmatter
Site builds successfully and contains example-post route
stories.test_command: test -d PROJECTS/example-post && test -f PROJECTS/example-post/draft.md && test -f SITE/src/content/blog/example-post.mdx && cd SITE && npm run build && test -d dist/blog/example-post && echo 'PASS'

stories.title: Create Skills Setup Script
stories.description: As a content creator I want to run scripts/setup-skills.sh to install skills so that the repo is portable to new machines.
acceptance_criteria: scripts/setup-skills.sh exists and is executable
Script creates ~/.claude/commands/ directory if needed
Script symlinks all PROMPTS/skills/*.md files
Running script twice is idempotent
stories.test_command: test -x scripts/setup-skills.sh && scripts/setup-skills.sh && test -L ~/.claude/commands/studio-ideate.md && test -L ~/.claude/commands/studio-draft.md && echo 'PASS'
